rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure that implementation-level bindings are placed in implementation annexes, not type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the input AADL model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions to avoid type errors.
Avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assign statements in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type-level guarantees or implementation-level assigns instead.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE variables unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about empty packages
